title: Slice of unpacked array with non-zero origin cause incorrect C++ code

features.name: unpacked array with non-zero origin
features.description: Code should include an unpacked array declaration with a non-zero origin (e.g., [3:6] instead of [0:6])

features.name: slice assignment on unpacked array with non-zero origin
features.description: Code should include a procedural slice assignment on an unpacked array where both source and destination use slice notation with non-zero origin
features.snippet:
logic unpack_sig1 [3:6];
always @(posedge clk) unpack_sig1[4:6] <= unpack_sig1[3:5];

features.name: slice operation with off-by-one index relative to origin
features.description: Code should include a slice operation where the slice indices minus the array origin exceed the allocated array size, triggering an index out-of-bounds error
features.snippet:
logic [3:6] unpack_sig1; // 4 elements
assign result = unpack_sig1[4:6]; // index 4 is out of bounds
